activities_projects.title: Sociological Deconstruction of Hiranya-garbha
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the intersection of ritual, caste fluidity, and political power
steps: Deconstruct the 'Hiranya-garbha' ritual as a tool for 'Sanskritization'
Analyze why the Rashtrakutas needed a 'symbolic rebirth' despite having military might
Investigate how the transition from 'Samanta' to 'Maharaja' challenged traditional Varna rules
Debate: 'Was the support of the Brahmanas more critical than the strength of the army for a new king?'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically evaluates how religious rituals were engineered to create political legitimacy
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis regarding social mobility and the role of the priestly class in state-making (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Historiographical Critique of Kalhana
activities_projects.objective: Compare medieval hagiography with critical historical inquiry
steps: Identify specific instances where Kalhana was critical of his contemporary rulers
Contrast Kalhana's methodology (use of inscriptions, coins, and earlier chronicles) with the anonymous writers of prashastis
Explain why Kalhana is often called the 'First Historian of India' in the modern sense
Write a critique on the limitations of using 'Court Poetry' as a primary source for history
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the emergence of critical historical consciousness in 12th-century Kashmir
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical comparison of evidence-based history versus panegyric poetry (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Geopolitics of the Tripartite Struggle
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the strategic and economic necessity of the Ganga valley
materials: Physical map of the Indo-Gangetic plain
steps: Evaluate the 'Resource Theory': Why was Kanauj the focal point for three distant empires?
Analyze the logistical challenges faced by the Rashtrakutas in campaigning so far from the Deccan
Trace how the constant warfare of the Tripartite struggle eventually weakened all three dynasties
Create a strategic brief for a King explaining why controlling the 'Doab' region is essential for empire-building
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes geography and economics to explain long-term military conflicts
activities_projects.evaluation: Strategic depth in explaining the collapse of the three dynasties due to over-extension (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Temples as Political Manifestos
activities_projects.objective: Analyze temple architecture as a display of 'Imperial Sovereignty'
steps: Analyze why Rajaraja I named the deity 'Rajarajeshvara' after himself
Investigate how the temple served as a 'microcosm' of the Chola state (collecting revenue, employing thousands)
Research the 'looting of idols' by Rajendra I and its symbolic meaning (taking the 'power' of the defeated king)
Explain the transition of temples from religious sites to economic corporations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates how kings used religion and grand architecture to consolidate and project power
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding the link between the 'Deity' and the 'King' in medieval ideology (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Lost Wax' Metallurgy Report
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the scientific precision of Chola bronze casting
steps: Explain the chemical property of the 'Lost Wax' process that prevents air bubbles in the final cast
Research why Chola bronzes are considered 'Hollow' vs 'Solid' and the technical difficulty of each
Discuss the aesthetic 'shastras' (rules) that artists had to follow to maintain divine proportions
Analyze the export of Chola bronze art to Southeast Asia through maritime trade
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects advanced scientific knowledge with artistic excellence in medieval South India
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical accuracy in describing the metallurgical steps and artistic constraints (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fiscal Policy: Vetti and the Agrarian Burden
activities_projects.objective: Critically examine the revenue systems of the Chola Empire
materials: List of 400 taxes from Chola inscriptions
steps: Differentiate between 'Vetti' (forced labor) and 'Kadamai' (land revenue) in terms of social impact
Analyze how the 'Brahmadeya' settlements acted as 'extension centers' for agriculture in forest lands
Evaluate the role of the 'Nadu' in tax collection and its level of autonomy from the central palace
Write a report on how the 'producing classes' (peasants and artisans) funded the 'monumental state'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the heavy taxation and labor structures that supported imperial grandeur
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical analysis of the economic relationship between the state and the peasantry (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Democratic Decentralization in Uttaramerur
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the limitations and strengths of the Sabha system
materials: Detailed text of the Uttaramerur Inscription
steps: Analyze the 'Disqualification Criteria' (e.g., failure to submit accounts) in the inscription
Debate: 'Was the Sabha truly democratic, or was it an oligarchy of land-owning Brahmanas?'
Explain the 'Kuda-volai' (pot-ticket) system as a mechanism to prevent corruption
Compare the Chola committee system with the modern Panchayati Raj system
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically assesses the nature of 'local self-government' in a monarchical era
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to identify the exclusive nature (caste/gender) of the 'Sabha' despite its democratic form (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Ghaznavid Perspective: Al-Biruni’s Ethnography
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the role of the scholar in an age of conquest
steps: Investigate Al-Biruni's objective: Why did he want to explain Hindu society to the Islamic world?
Analyze his observations on the 'caste system' and 'scientific knowledge' in India
Discuss the ethics of a scholar traveling with a conqueror (Mahmud of Ghazni)
Evaluate Al-Biruni’s use of Sanskrit sources as a method of 'Intellectual Diplomacy'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appraises the value of cross-cultural documentation in preserving historical data
activities_projects.evaluation: Insight into Al-Biruni's methodology and his neutral vs. biased observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hydraulic Engineering in the Kaveri Delta
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the environmental management strategies of the Cholas
materials: Topographical maps of the Kaveri delta
steps: Analyze the design of the 'Anicut' (dams) and how they managed the silt-carrying branches of the Kaveri
Explain the 'Artificial Rainwater Harvesting' systems mentioned in the text
Investigate the maintenance of canals—who was responsible for 'desilting' (Sabha or King?)
Argue how surplus food from these systems enabled the Cholas to maintain a massive standing army
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates civil engineering and environmental control to the stability of an empire
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical understanding of irrigation and its link to political economy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tarain: The Turning Point
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the shift in military tactics and its long-term impact
materials: Strategic layout of the 1191 and 1192 battles
steps: Compare the military composition of the Chahamanas (elephants/infantry) vs. Ghorid Turks (horse archers)
Analyze why Prithviraj III’s victory in 1191 did not prevent his defeat in 1192
Investigate the role of 'feudal levies' versus 'standing professional armies' in the outcome
Assess the Second Battle of Tarain as the foundational event for the Delhi Sultanate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the military and political failures that led to the end of early medieval kingdoms
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining the tactical shift from Rajput to Turkish warfare (10 marks)
